title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 10 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond on 10 February 2026 to finance its foreign currency reserves, marking the first operation under its updated issuance programme. The bond carries a 3.5% coupon and matures 18 February 2029, with four joint lead managers: RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley. The Bank shifted to a structured timetable of two benchmark issuances annually in September 2025, providing greater transparency in its reserve financing operations.
key_points: Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year RegS/144A eurobond on 10 February 2026 for foreign currency reserve financing
Bond coupon is 3.5% per annum with maturity on 18 February 2029
Joint Lead Managers: RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
Bank transitioned to two benchmark issuances annually as of September 2025 announcement
Bank of England FX reserves are separate from and managed independently of Government FX reserves
This was the first 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme
why_it_matters: The Bank of England's move to a regularized, transparent issuance schedule for financing its foreign currency reserves represents a structural change in how a major central bank accesses capital markets. The shift from irregular operations to predictable semi-annual benchmark issuances reduces market uncertainty and provides a consistent reference point for investors in central bank debt. This affects how reserve managers and sovereign debt desks position for central bank paper supply.
macro_context: Central bank foreign currency reserve management through domestic bond issuance affects global FX liquidity and sovereign debt market dynamics. The Bank of England's structured issuance programme represents a policy-level approach to reserve financing that could influence other central banks' practices.
market_structure_context: The formalization of a two-benchmark-annual issuance timetable with designated market makers (joint lead managers) establishes a more structured framework for sovereign/reserve-related bond distribution. This includes explicit product governance targeting (ECP/Professionals only, MiFIR/MiFID II compliance), indicating standardized market infrastructure for reserve financing instruments.

issues_found: The important_numbers field includes 3.75 (Bank Rate), which is not present in this specific market notice. The Bank Rate appears in a related navigation link (MPC summary from 19 March 2026), not in the source body of this notice.

notes: Source is a legitimate Bank of England market notice but content is thin, dominated by legal disclaimers and distribution restrictions. Core factual content about the bond issuance is verifiable and specific. The shift to regularized issuance announced in September 2025 is the most structurally significant element for market structure research. The Bank Rate of 3.75% appears in related navigation content (MPC summary link) but is not part of this specific market notice.
